headline: Green dreams shattered
subtext: Council's green energy initiative turns into a waste of ratepayers' money as the solar panels installed on council buildings are found to be faulty, leading to a costly repair bill and mounting public outrage. Meanwhile, the council continues to push through its infrastructure projects without considering their impact on local residents and wildlife.
body:
Wannabesville - In an unprecedented move by the local council, hopes for a solar-powered future have been utterly dashed. The announcement came during last week's Council Chambers meeting, where councillors unanimously rejected any plans for rooftop solar installations.

Cr Mary-Jane O'Leary led the charge, citing safety concerns as her main reason to oppose such initiatives. "Do you really want these shiny things on your roof? What if they fall off and smash your grandmother's prized teacup?"

The decision has sparked outrage among Wannabesville residents who had hoped for a more eco-friendly community. Protesters have been spotted congregating outside the Council Chambers, shouting slogans such as "When in doubt, go solar!" and "Cr O'Leary is no sun hero!"

Local Ratepayer John Smith expressed his disappointment: "I thought we were trying to reduce our carbon footprint around here. But I guess it's back to business as usual."

The Steyne Pub remained unperturbed by the news - according to their spokesperson, "As long as our beer stays cold, we don't really care about solar or no-solar."

With this decision, Wannabesville has effectively put its green dreams on hold. For now, residents will have to content themselves with the sight of power lines strung across the horizon - unless you happen to be in The Corso shopping district, where a solar-powered B-Line bus awaits.

Stay tuned for updates from our roo-

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.
